title: vicia canescens subsp. canescens
intro: Vicia canescens subsp. canescens, also known as the hoary vetch or woolly vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its hairy stems and white or purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Vicia canescens subsp. canescens include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. The herbal preparations of Vicia canescens subsp. canescens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
